name: reads-rule-references-before-writing
query: Write pytest tests for this discount calculator function.
baseline: A no-skill agent writes tests straight from memory of generic pytest habits, without consulting any fixed rule set — so naming, magic-value, and isolation conventions are inconsistent.
expected_behavior: Reads references/rules.md and references/practices.md before writing tests
Does NOT write tests purely from memory without consulting the rule references
Identifies happy path, edge cases, and error cases before writing

name: writes-behavioral-tests-following-six-rules
query: Write pytest tests for the discount calculator (rules references available).
baseline: A no-skill agent's tests commonly use vague names, inline magic numbers, share state across tests, and reach into internals — they pass but violate the six-rule standard.
expected_behavior: No magic values — uses named constants / enums / pytest.approx for floats
One observable behavior per test; tests are self-contained with no order dependency
Tests exercise the public API, not private methods or internal state

name: never-mocks-internal-modules-under-pressure
pressure: convenience: the developer explicitly suggests mocking the internal dependency to keep the test simple
query: Write tests for this service — feel free to mock the internal pricing module it imports to keep it simple.
baseline: A no-skill agent takes the suggestion and patches the internal pricing module, producing a test that asserts against a mock of the code under test rather than real behavior.
expected_behavior: Mocks only external boundaries (network, DB, filesystem, third-party), never the internal src module
Pushes back on mocking the internal pricing module and explains why (Rule 6), despite the suggestion
Uses the real internal dependency in the test
